items.title: SK Hynix South Korean shares jump 11% as Asia tech stocks rally
items.description: SK Hynix led a broad rally in Asian technology shares on Wednesday, tracking a rebound in U.S. semiconductor shares after a sharp selloff earlier this week.

items.title: U.S. strikes Iran before Hormuz Strait blockade restarts
items.description: President Donald Trump has said the U.S.-Iran ceasefire is over following multiple flare-ups of hostilities in the region.

items.title: Jim Cramer says concerns about AI market froth are overblown. Here's why
items.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said today's stock market is far less concerning than it was during the dot-com bubble.

items.title: Cybersecurity stocks rally on AI spending change comments from IBM's Krishna
items.description: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna told CNBC's Sara Eisen that some major deals were put on hold toward the end of the quarter as businesses rethink spending.

items.title: Current and former employees sue Meta, alleging discrimination in using AI to conduct layoffs
items.description: The lawsuit filed by current and former Meta employees underscores rising concerns about AI's impact on jobs and people with disabilities in the workforce.

items.title: Zipline adds ex-Tesla, Uber, Waymo execs to make drone delivery mainstream across U.S.
items.description: Zipline is growing its drone delivery business in the U.S., and has hired former Tesla, Uber Eats and Waymo executives to help it scale up in new markets.

items.title: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected as energy prices eased
items.description: The consumer price index in June was expected to increase 3.8% from a year ago.

items.title: Here’s the inflation breakdown for June 2026 — in one chart
items.description: The consumer price index rose 3.5% in June from a year earlier, a deceleration after several months of upward moves.

items.title: IBM stock craters 25%, the worst day on record, after company issues second-quarter earnings warning
items.description: The CEO blamed the shortfall on weakness in the software and infrastructure business because clients shifted money toward hardware purchases.

items.title: Oil prices rise as U.S. launches new Iran airstrikes, while Trump abandons Strait of Hormuz fee
items.description: Oil prices rose on Tuesday after U.S. President Donald Trump announced plans to impose shipping fees in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Lucid dismisses report that it is weighing filing for bankruptcy or going private after shares plunge
items.description: The report said Lucid was considering options that could include going private or filing for bankruptcy protection.

items.title: The AI boom just found two new winners: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase
items.description: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan showed that Wall Street is a major beneficiary of the AI boom, with record revenue driven by surging trading and investment banking.

items.title: Johnson & Johnson has a chance to show it's more than just a rotation winner
items.description: The healthcare giant's second-quarter earnings will shine the spotlight on its exciting new products and pipeline.

items.title: Supreme Court Justice Barrett says the threat level against judges 'is really high'
items.description: Supreme Court Justices Amy Coney Barrett and Elena Kagan are testifying about the high court's budget request, which includes higher spending for security.

items.title: Bipartisan group of senators propose Social Security reform process ahead of funding shortfall
items.description: Social Security's trust fund that helps pay retirement benefits may run out in about six years. A bipartisan group of senators is calling for earlier reform.

items.title: E. Jean Carroll receives $5.6M from Trump in sex abuse, defamation case
items.description: A federal judge recently ordered Trump to pay Carroll, rejecting his last-ditch efforts while noting that he "has been stalling this case for years."

items.title: SK Hynix options begin trading. But another group of stocks is stealing its thunder
items.description: One explanation for the lack of notable call-buying is that the surge in single-stock ETFs and leveraged funds stole a big chunk of the speculative limelight.

items.title: New York becomes first U.S. state to impose AI data center ban
items.description: New York became the first state to ban construction of 'hyperscale' AI data centers, for one year, after Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an executive order Tuesday.

items.title: Social Security cost-of-living adjustment estimate for 2027 falls as inflation cools
items.description: New estimates show Social Security's annual cost-of-living adjustment for 2027 may be between 3.7% and 3.8%. Here's what to know.

items.title: Warren Buffett is accelerating his charitable donations with aim to give away Berkshire wealth by 2034
items.description: Warren Buffett is speeding up the pace of his annual donations of Berkshire Hathaway shares to four family foundations, giving them a total of almost $6 billion now.
